title: Head worn wireless computer having a display suitable for use as a mobile internet device
abstract: A handheld wireless display device, having at least SVGA-type resolution, includes a wireless interface, such as Bluetoothâ¢, WiFiâ¢, Wimaxâ¢, cellular or satellite, to allow the device to utilize a number of different hosts, such as a cell phone, personal computer, media player. The display may be monocular or binocular. Input mechanisms, such as switches, scroll wheels, touch pads, allow selection and navigation of menus, playing media files, setting volume and screen brightness/contrast, activating host remote controls or performing other commands. The device may include MIM diodes, Hall effect sensors, or other position transducers and/or accelerometers to detect lateral movements along and rotational gestures around the X, Y and Z axes as gesture inputs and movement queues. These commands may change pages, scroll up, down or across an enlarged screen image, such as for web browsing. An embedded software driver (e.g., Microsoft Windows SideShowâ¢) permits replicating a high-resolution screen display from a host PC. The device may repeatedly poll the host at intervals for updated content even when the host is powered off, asleep or hibernating, and may return the host to its previous power state.
claims: 1. A head mounted electronic device comprising: a head mounted support having an arm, the head mounted support being configured to be worn by a user; a display coupled to the arm of the head mounted support; a processor and a memory coupled to the display; one or more movement tracking inputs coupled to the display, wherein at least one of the one or more movement tracking inputs are configured to detect one or more hand gestures, and wherein at least one of the one or more hand gestures are configured to cause a response of an output of the display; a wireless transceiver coupled to the processor, wherein the wireless transceiver employs a Bluetooth® physical layer with a Bluetooth® proxy to implement a packet switching gateway, wherein the wireless transceiver is configured to receive a signal consisting of audio and visual information received over a first radio frequency channel and consisting of non-audio and non-visual information received over a second radio frequency channel, and wherein the visual information of the signal is of at least Video Graphic Array (VGA) quality and is received over a Serial Port Profile (SPP) Bluetooth wireless connection; an audio input device coupled to the processor; an audio output device coupled to the processor; and a speech recognition engine executable by the processor to allow for audio commands to be received by the audio input device and executed by the processor.
